title: NURSE EDUCATION: UTILIZING ACETAMINOPHEN AND NSAIDS TO REDUCE POST-OPERATIVE OPIOID CONSUMPTION AFTER ORTHOPEDIC SURGERY
author: David Sierra
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The purpose of this QI project was to develop a post anesthesia care unit (PACU) nurse educational intervention to enhance their knowledge of non-opioid multimodal analgesia, acetaminophen and non steroidal anti-inflammatory drugs (NSAIDS), as it pertains to the ERAS protocol to control post-orthopedic surgery patients’ pain. The aim was to assess PACU nurses’ attitudes toward adopting new evidence-based practice and identify gaps in their knowledge of the ERAS pain management protocol using the evidence-based practice attitudes scale (EBPAS) in combination with a qualitative and socio-demographic variable questionnaire. Utilizing a descriptive cross-sectional design, with the assistance of the IOWA model as a theoretical framework, the information gathered in this project led to the successful development of an educational PowerPoint presentation targeting the unique knowledge gaps of this sample of PACU nurses. Supporting nurses doubtful of ERAS protocol efficacy through knowledge enhancing education may empower them to accept ERAS protocols leading to decreased opioid use.
